entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A staple crop for arid and semi-arid regions, described as a climate resilient nutricereal.

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Environmental regions where pearl millet is a staple crop.

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENT NUTRICEREAL
entities.description: A classification of pearl millet based on its resilience to climate change and nutritional value.

entities.name: BOLD SEEDED
entities.description: An agronomic trait of pearl millet genotypes used in breeding strategies.

entities.name: SEMI-DWARF
entities.description: An agronomic trait of pearl millet genotypes used in breeding strategies.

entities.name: EARLY FLOWERING
entities.description: An agronomic trait of pearl millet genotypes used in breeding strategies.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: A key breeding target trait to be enhanced in pearl millet.

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: A key breeding target trait to be enhanced in pearl millet.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Complex traits of pearl millet inbreds that can be dissected through QTL mapping.

entities.name: QTL MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to dissect complex agronomic traits by identifying quantitative trait loci.

entities.name: FLOWERING TIME
entities.description: An agronomically important trait mapped in the RIL population of pearl millet.

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: An agronomically important trait mapped in the RIL population of pearl millet.

entities.name: PANICLE LENGTH
entities.description: An agronomically important trait mapped in the RIL population of pearl millet.

entities.name: GRAIN WEIGHT
entities.description: An agronomically important trait mapped in the RIL population of pearl millet.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINE POPULATION
entities.description: The specific population used to map QTLs for agronomic traits.

entities.name: ICMB 841-P3 X 863B-P2 CROSS
entities.description: The genetic cross used to develop the recombinant inbred line population.

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variation due to genetic factors for traits like FT, PH, and PL.

entities.name: QTL
entities.description: Quantitative trait loci detected on chromosomes controlling specific agronomic traits.

entities.name: CHROMOSOME
entities.description: The genomic structures on which QTLs are located.

entities.name: LG3
entities.description: A linkage group containing a major QTL common for flowering time and plant height.

entities.name: LG1
entities.description: A linkage group where a major QTL for panicle length was detected.

entities.name: LG2
entities.description: A linkage group where a major QTL for panicle length was detected.

entities.name: LG6B
entities.description: A linkage group where major QTLs for panicle length and grain weight were detected.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC VARIANCE
entities.description: The variance (R2) explained by a specific QTL.

entities.name: DIGENIC INTERACTIONS
entities.description: Interactions between two genes affecting traits like flowering time and panicle length.

entities.name: EPISTATIC EFFECTS
entities.description: Genetic interactions that were analyzed for QQE interactions.

entities.name: QQE INTERACTIONS
entities.description: QTL x QTL x environment interactions.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding program method that utilizes mapped QTLs for selection.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding program method that utilizes mapped QTLs for selection.
